manifest.name: zeroclaw-android
manifest.description: First native zeroclaw build on Android/Termux. aarch64. No proot. No emulation.

host_environment.ollama_notes: Running locally, limited by device RAM — model selection critical

parent_project.name: Vertical AI
parent_project.description: Terminal-based multi-persona AI boardroom running entirely on Android via Termux + Ollama. Personas debate ideas, analyze markets, evolve business models, and visualize everything in a 3D city (Three.js).
parent_project.status: Active development — MVP phases 1-4 complete, UX and frontend are current pain points

architecture.boardroom: Multi-persona orchestration layer (Chairman, Adversary, Architect, CEO, CTO, CMO, CFO, UX Designer, Swarm, Rap Genius, Pytch, Twoie)
architecture.swarm: Scout agents for market data, competitor intelligence, trend signals
architecture.dna: Genetic algorithm layer — business models encoded as strings that mutate and evolve
architecture.code_city: Three.js 3D visualization — buildings represent agents/modules, glow on activity
architecture.ollama: Local LLM backend — no cloud, all on-device

known_pain_points: Frontend UX is not where it needs to be — hard to explain but the experience doesn't match the vision
Backend not producing the right kind of UX response — output format and flow feel off
Ollama model limitations — 1.5B param models too weak for complex multi-persona reasoning
asyncio event loop conflicts between concurrent persona modules
3D Code City visualization planned but not yet live-connected to backend

zeroclaw_role.what_it_is: Nostr protocol client/relay tool — handles decentralized communication, relay pool management, and key operations over the Nostr network
potential_integration_roles: Decentralized message bus between Vertical AI personas — route boardroom decisions over Nostr relays
Persistent event log — publish boardroom session events as Nostr events for tamper-evident history
Swarm intelligence data channel — scouts publish findings as Nostr events, boardroom subscribes
External comms layer — publish AI outputs to Nostr network for public/team consumption
Code City event feed — zeroclaw publishes activity events that Code City visualizes as building pulses
zeroclaw_role.integration_status: Binary compiled and available. Not yet wired into Vertical AI stack. Next step is defining the interface layer.

integration_guidance_for_claude.context: You are being handed this manifest to help integrate zeroclaw into the Vertical AI / ModMind / Code City stack. The human you are working with is BleakNarratives — solo developer, working entirely on Android/Termux, building a multi-persona AI boardroom with 3D visualization. He is technical, moves fast, doesn't like over-explanation, and has strong vision.
immediate_priorities: Fix the UX disconnect — the frontend experience does not match the ambition of the system. Focus on making boardroom output feel visceral and alive, not like a log dump.
Help define how zeroclaw plugs in as a communication/event layer without breaking existing asyncio architecture
Keep everything Termux-compatible — no Docker, no cloud dependencies, no root required
approach_notes: BleakNarratives works best with concrete, runnable code — not architecture diagrams
He will push through problems but needs momentum — don't let him get stuck in config hell
The vision is clear; execution and polish are where help is needed
When in doubt, ship something that works at 80% over a perfect solution that blocks progress
He already has: working Ollama backend, persona system, basic boardroom TUI, Code City skeleton, zeroclaw binary
do_not: Suggest proot or emulation — he specifically achieved native builds to avoid this
Recommend cloud-first solutions — this is intentionally offline-capable
Over-engineer the architecture — complexity is the enemy here
Repeat context he already knows — he's deep in this stack
suggested_first_questions: What does the current boardroom output actually look like vs what you want it to feel like?
Is the UX issue in the terminal rendering (Rich/curses), the response format from Ollama, or the persona orchestration flow?
Do you want zeroclaw as an internal event bus or as an external-facing publish layer?

molt_and_moltbook.description: Molt is referenced as the orchestrator in the Vertical AI stack. MoltBook appears to be a related notebook/session management layer. Both run in Termux.
molt_and_moltbook.status: Active — was the primary focus before zeroclaw integration sprint
molt_and_moltbook.notes: Ollama servers handling Molt traffic will face load issues as usage grows — scalability is a known concern

session_history_summary.what_happened_today: BleakNarratives attempted to compile zeroclaw v0.1.7 on Android/Termux. Gemini CLI and Gemini Android app both failed. After approximately 6 terminal crashes, OOM kills during linking, and a battery scare at 21%, the build succeeded at 04:11:47 CST on 2026-02-25 using mold linker + cargo profile optimizations. This is believed to be the first native zeroclaw build on Android. The binary and build documentation were pushed to GitHub.
session_history_summary.key_breakthrough: Replacing the default linker with mold and setting codegen-units=1 + lto=thin + opt-level=z reduced peak RAM enough for the link step to complete without being OOM-killed by Android
